CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'abc'), (2, 'def'), (3, 'abcde'), (4, 'xyz');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a%';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE '%b%';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a_c';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a%' AND id > 2;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'x%');
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (id INT, description VARCHAR(200));
INSERT INTO test_table2 (id, description) VALUES (1, 'This is abc'), (2, 'This is def'), (3, 'abc description');
SELECT t1.*, t2.* FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.name LIKE 'a%' AND t2.description LIKE '%abc%';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name NOT LIKE 'xyz';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a%' OR name LIKE 'd%';
CREATE TABLE test_table3 (id INT, category VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_table3 (id, category) VALUES (1, 'Category abc'), (2, 'Category def');
SELECT * FROM test_table3 WHERE category LIKE 'Category%';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a%';
SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE (SELECT CONCAT('a', '%') FROM dual) LIMIT 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table2 WHERE description LIKE '%abc%' AND test_table.id = test_table2.id);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table3 WHERE category LIKE 'Category%');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'a%' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table2 WHERE description LIKE 'def%');
SELECT t1.name, t2.description FROM test_table t1, test_table2 t2 WHERE t1.name LIKE 'abc%' AND t2.description LIKE 'This is%';
